title: Respiratory medication review

red_flags.text: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.
red_flags.rationale: Supports asthma-control monitoring for respiratory medication review; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma

medication.text: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
medication.rationale: Supports structured clinician-led medicine review for respiratory medication review; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

medication.text: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.
medication.rationale: Supports benefit-harm and preference discussion for respiratory medication review; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — involvement, values, preferences, benefits, and harms

referral.text: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.
referral.rationale: Supports COPD follow-up context for respiratory medication review; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Diagnosis, medicine, device, dose, schedule, adherence, technique, benefit, adverse effects, interactions, control, decision and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for respiratory medication review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
